task_completion.description: SolveSpace is opened and a new 2D sketch is active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SolveSpace GUI is visible with a blank or active 2D sketching canvas.

task_completion.description: A line segment representing the crank is drawn starting from the origin.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A line segment is visible on the canvas with one endpoint constrained to the origin (0,0), typically marked by the intersection of the coordinate axes.

task_completion.description: The crank is constrained to 25 mm in length and 45 degrees from the horizontal axis.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visible constraint labels on or near the crank line showing '25' (or similar length indicator) and '45' (angle indicator relative to the horizontal axis).

task_completion.description: A connecting rod is drawn and attached to the free end of the crank.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A second line segment is connected to the non-origin endpoint of the crank line, forming a continuous linkage.

task_completion.description: The connecting rod is constrained to 80 mm and its free endpoint is constrained to the horizontal axis.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A length constraint label of '80' is visible on the connecting rod, and its far endpoint lies exactly on the horizontal axis (Y=0), often indicated by a point-on-line constraint or coordinate alignment.

task_completion.description: The sketch is saved to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SolveSpace window title bar shows 'slider_crank.slvs' or a save dialog confirms the path '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/slider_crank.slvs'.

integrity.description: The agent used the SolveSpace GUI to create the sketch.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the SolveSpace application interface being actively used (e.g., menus open, tools selected, constraints being placed), rather than just terminal commands.

integrity.description: The sketch was built progressively rather than opened from a pre-existing file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Sequential screenshots show the step-by-step addition of lines and constraints, starting from a blank canvas.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the GUI by generating the .slvs file via terminal scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal is not used to run python scripts, echo base64 strings, or copy pre-made files to the target directory to create the .slvs file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file path is '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/slider_crank.slvs'. The crank must be 25.0 mm long at a 45.0-degree angle, placing the crank pin at approximately (17.68, 17.68). The connecting rod must be 80.0 mm long, placing the slider on the horizontal axis at X ≈ 95.70. A tolerance of 0.5 mm is acceptable for these coordinates.
